message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #1 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: HIGH_STAKES tier has no inner timeout/cancellation — hung subprocess calls permanently leak the global pool's worker slots, killing reasoning process-wide for ALL concurrent studies
DIMENSION: concurrency & load
SEVERITY: HIGH
FILE: src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py 181-217, 361-439, 442-490
DESCRIPTION: The reasoning seam routes every LLM call through a single module-global, 8-worker ThreadPoolExecutor (_pool) shared across all threads and all concurrent run_study invocations. _with_timeout enforces only a WALL-CLOCK timeout via future.result(timeout=...); it explicitly does NOT cancel the worker (the docstring at lines 226-229 acknowledges a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked in a backend call). The slot is only released by the add_done_callback (_release), i.e. when the underlying call actually returns. For the BULK tier this is survivable because _bulk_complete passes client_kwargs={'timeout': BULK_TIMEOUT} to ChatOllama, so the worker is guaranteed to return and free its slot. But the HIGH_STAKES tier (_high_stakes_complete) passes NO timeout to ClaudeAgentOptions or query() and wraps the claude subprocess in anyio.run(_drain) with no cancellation. If the claude CLI subprocess hangs (stalled subscription endpoint, network blackhole, child process wedged), the worker thread blocks forever and its pool slot is NEVER released. _inflight stays elevated permanently. Once 8 such high-stakes calls hang — entirely plausible across concurrent studies when the subscription path stalls — the global pool is permanently saturated: _submit_bounded raises RateLimitError for EVERY subsequent call (any tier, any study), and _run_with_retries exhausts the rate-limit budget and fails the caller permanently. There is no recovery short of restarting the process. PoC confirmed: (1) 8 hung high-stakes-style calls drive seam._inflight to 8 and it never returns to 0; (2) an unrelated, healthy Study B issuing a fast call gets RateLimitError instead of running ('Study B cannot reason at all despite its own backend being fine'); (3) with _inflight forced to 8, seam._run_with_retries() on a fresh BULK request fails with RateLimitError after exhausting all rate retries in ~0.4s. This is a process-wide, cross-study, unrecoverable resource-exhaustion (effectively a livelock/deadlock of the whole reasoning subsystem) triggered by the most critical/gated tier.
SUGGESTED FIX: Give the HIGH_STAKES path a real, enforceable timeout so a hung subprocess cannot permanently hold a worker. Run query()/_drain under anyio's move_on_after/fail_after (or asyncio.wait_for) bounded by HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT inside _high_stakes_complete, and ensure the claude subprocess is actually terminated on cancellation (the worker must RETURN so _release fires and the slot frees). Additionally, consider isolating the high-stakes tier in its own bounded pool so a high-stakes stall cannot starve bulk-tier callers, and/or have _release/health logic reclaim slots whose worker has exceeded a hard ceiling. Optionally surface a metric/alert when _inflight stays pinned at _POOL_WORKERS.
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll investigate this claim by reading the actual code in the reasoning seam.

content.content:
1	"""Tiered reasoning seam — the single abstraction over LLM execution by stakes tier.
2	
3	No engine component talks to an LLM directly; everything routes through :class:`ReasoningSeam`.
4	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's hardened ``reasoning_client`` (ARCHITECTURE.md
5	§1, ADR-0003). Two execution tiers:
6	
7	- ``StakesTier.BULK`` — a local model runtime (loopback-only, e.g. Ollama) for high-volume,
8	  low-stakes work.
9	- ``StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES`` — a frontier model via a **fixed-cost subscription** path
10	  (the ``claude`` CLI / Agent SDK using an OAuth token). No metered API key is ever used:
11	  every metered Anthropic credential is scrubbed from the subprocess environment first.
12	
13	Hardened invariants preserved from the prior platform:
14	
15	- forward-proof credential scrubbing (any ``ANTHROPIC_*KEY``/``*TOKEN`` stripped; the
16	  subscription ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` preserved);
17	- hard per-call timeouts via a bounded worker pool with a saturation guard;
18	- independent retry budgets — exponential backoff for transient errors, long backpressure
19	  for rate limits — and permanent errors never retried;
20	- robust JSON extraction via a balanced-brace walker (not a greedy regex);
21	- loopback enforcement on the local backend (ADR-0003).
22	
23	Heavy backends (``langchain_ollama``, ``claude_agent_sdk``) and tracing (``opentelemetry``)
24	are imported lazily, so this module imports — and is unit-testable by mocking ``_attempt`` —
25	with none of them installed. Install the optional ``reasoning`` extra to run real calls.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import json
31	import os
32	import threading
33	import time
34	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
35	from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeout
36	from contextlib import contextmanager
37	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
38	from enum import Enum
39	from pathlib import Path
40	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol, cast, runtime_checkable
41	
42	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
43	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_url
44	
45	
46	class StakesTier(Enum):
47	    BULK = "bulk"  # local model — high volume, low stakes
48	    HIGH_STAKES = "high"  # frontier model via fixed-cost subscription — gated, traced
49	
50	
51	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
52	class ReasoningRequest:
53	    prompt: str
54	    tier: StakesTier
55	    purpose: str
56	    params: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
57	
58	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
59	        object.__setattr__(self, "params", freeze_mapping(self.params))
60	
61	
62	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	# Errors
64	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
65	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
66	    """Transient reasoning failure (eligible for retry)."""
67	
68	
69	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
70	    """Non-transient failure — never retried (bad model, auth, saturation)."""
71	
72	
73	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
74	    """Rate-limit / capacity signal — retried with long backpressure, not fast backoff."""
75	
76	
77	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
78	# Config (dataset-agnostic env contract, ASSAY_* prefix)
79	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
80	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
81	    val = os.environ.get(name)
82	    return val if val is not None and val != "" else default
83	
84	
85	def _truthy(name: str) -> bool:
86	    return os.environ.get(name, "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
87	
88	
89	BULK_MODEL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_MODEL", "llama3.1:8b")
90	BULK_BASE_URL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
91	HIGH_STAKES_MODEL = os.environ.get("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_MODEL") or None
92	
93	BULK_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_BULK_TIMEOUT", "120")))
94	# Cap BULK generation length. ChatOllama/Ollama default to unbounded (-1); without a cap a
95	# local model can run away to context exhaustion, and the wall-clock timeout cannot interrupt a
96	# call already blocked in the backend (a leaked pool slot). Bounding output tokens is the real
97	# guard (LangChain's own ChatOllama example sets num_predict explicitly).
98	BULK_NUM_PREDICT = max(1, int(_env("ASSAY_BULK_NUM_PREDICT", "2048")))
99	HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT", "300")))
100	
101	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
102	BACKOFF_BASE = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5")))
103	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30")))
104	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
105	# run_json re-rolls on a PARSE failure. This is independent of the transient/rate-limit retry
106	# budget above (which applies per generation) so the two do not multiply into a retry storm; an
107	# overall wall-clock deadline bounds the total regardless (#102).
108	JSON_REROLLS = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_JSON_REROLLS", "2")))
109	RUN_JSON_DEADLINE = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RUN_JSON_DEADLINE", "600")))
110	
111	# OAuth subscription token name (external CLI contract — not renamed).
112	OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV = "CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"
113	
114	
115	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
116	# Credential scrubbing (ADR-0003 / no metered API) — pure, forward-proof
117	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
118	def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
119	    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK would read as a METERED credential.
120	
121	    Matches the *shape* — any ``ANTHROPIC_*`` var naming a ``KEY`` or ``TOKEN`` — rather than
122	    a static list, so a newly-added metered var cannot silently slip through. The subscription
123	    ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` does not start with ``ANTHROPIC_`` and is deliberately kept.
124	    """
125	    return key.startswith("ANTHROPIC_") and ("KEY" in key or "TOKEN" in key)
126	
127	
128	# Env vars that — beyond metered credentials — would redirect the subprocess OFF-BOX or onto a
129	# metered provider, defeating ADR-0003 / "no metered API". The claude CLI honours all of these.
130	_UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS = frozenset(
131	    {
132	        "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL",
133	        "ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL",
134	        "ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL",
135	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK",
136	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX",
137	        "CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER",
138	        "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS",
139	    }
140	)
141	
142	
143	def is_unsafe_subprocess_var(key: str) -> bool:
144	    """True for any env var that must not reach the high-stakes subprocess (#101).
145	
146	    Covers metered credentials, off-box/metered-provider redirects, proxies (which redirect
147	    egress), and cloud-provider credentials (AWS_*/GOOGLE_*). The subscription OAuth token is
148	    deliberately NOT matched.
149	    """
150	    ku = key.upper()
151	    return (
152	        is_metered_anthropic_credential(key)
153	        or ku in _UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS
154	        or ku.endswith("_PROXY")  # HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/ALL_PROXY/NO_PROXY (any case)
155	        or ku.startswith("AWS_")
156	        or ku.startswith("GOOGLE_")
157	    )
158	
159	
160	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
161	    """The process env hardened for the high-stakes subprocess (ADR-0003 / no metered API).
162	
163	    Every unsafe var (:func:`is_unsafe_subprocess_var`) is **overwritten to empty**, not merely
164	    omitted. The claude-agent-sdk builds the child env as ``{**os.environ, **options.env}`` — it
165	    *merges* over the inherited environment — so a dropped key would silently fall through to its
166	    inherited value (the metered key / off-box redirect would survive). Setting it to ``""`` in
167	    the dict we hand over guarantees the merge overwrites the inherited value (#101). The
168	    subscription OAuth token is preserved so the fixed-cost path still authenticates.
169	    """
170	    env = dict(os.environ)
171	    for k in list(env):
172	        if is_unsafe_subprocess_var(k):
173	            env[k] = ""
174	    return env
175	
176	
177	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
178	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
179	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
180	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
181	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
182	_inflight = 0
183	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
184	
185	
186	def _release(_future: Any) -> None:
187	    global _inflight
188	    with _inflight_lock:
189	        _inflight -= 1
190	
191	
192	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
193	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
194	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
195	    global _inflight
196	    with _inflight_lock:
197	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
198	            # Saturation is a TRANSIENT condition — surface a retryable backpressure signal so
199	            # _run_with_retries waits and re-tries rather than failing the caller permanently
200	            # (#104). Concurrent callers thus queue briefly instead of erroring out.
201	            raise RateLimitError(
202	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
203	                "backpressure; retry shortly"
204	            )
205	        _inflight += 1
206	    try:
207	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
208	    except RuntimeError as exc:  # pool shut down at interpreter exit — normalize, don't leak (#116)
209	        with _inflight_lock:
210	            _inflight -= 1
211	        raise PermanentReasoningError(f"reasoning pool is shut down: {exc}") from exc
212	    except BaseException:
213	        with _inflight_lock:
214	            _inflight -= 1
215	        raise
216	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
217	    return future
218	
219	
220	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
221	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
222	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
223	    try:
224	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
225	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
226	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
227	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
228	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
229	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
230	
231	
232	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
233	# JSON extraction — balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
234	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
235	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
236	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
237	
238	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
239	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
240	    """
241	    start = None
242	    opener = closer = ""
243	    depth = 0
244	    in_string = False
245	    escape = False
246	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
247	        if start is None:
248	            if ch in "{[":
249	                start, opener = i, ch
250	                closer = "}" if ch == "{" else "]"
251	                depth = 1
252	            continue
253	        if in_string:
254	            if escape:
255	                escape = False
256	            elif ch == "\\":
257	                escape = True
258	            elif ch == '"':
259	                in_string = False
260	            continue
261	        if ch == '"':
262	            in_string = True
263	        elif ch == opener:
264	            depth += 1
265	        elif ch == closer:
266	            depth -= 1
267	            if depth == 0:
268	                return text[start : i + 1]
269	    return None
270	
271	
272	def extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
273	    """Parse JSON from a model reply (handles code fences and surrounding prose)."""
274	    span = _first_balanced_json(text)
275	    if span is None:
276	        raise ReasoningError("no JSON object/array found in reply")
277	    try:
278	        return json.loads(span)
279	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
280	        raise ReasoningError(f"reply contained malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc
281	
282	
283	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
284	# Tier backends (lazy imports — module loads without them)
285	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
286	def _bulk_complete(
287	    prompt: str,
288	    system: str | None,
289	    temperature: float,
290	    model: str,
291	    json_mode: bool = False,
292	    *,
293	    seed: int | None = None,
294	    json_schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
295	) -> str:
296	    """Tier: BULK — local model runtime (loopback-enforced).
297	
298	    ``num_predict`` is always set (bounded generation). For JSON, a caller-supplied
299	    ``json_schema`` uses Ollama's schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path); otherwise
300	    ``json_mode`` falls back to loose ``format="json"``. ``seed`` lets a retry force a different
301	    deterministic decode without sharpening temperature.
302	    """
303	    require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
304	    try:
305	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
306	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
307	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
308	            "bulk tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (langchain-ollama) — not installed"
309	        ) from exc
310	
311	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = dict(
312	        model=model,
313	        base_url=BULK_BASE_URL,
314	        temperature=temperature,
315	        num_predict=BULK_NUM_PREDICT,  # bound generation length (H1)
316	        client_kwargs={"timeout": BULK_TIMEOUT},
317	    )
318	    if seed is not None:
319	        kwargs["seed"] = seed
320	    if json_schema is not None:
321	        kwargs["format"] = json_schema  # Ollama schema-constrained decoding (reliable JSON)
322	    elif json_mode:
323	        kwargs["format"] = "json"  # loose native JSON decoding (syntactic only)
324	    client = ChatOllama(**kwargs)
325	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
326	    if system:
327	        messages.append(("system", system))
328	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
329	    try:
330	        reply = client.invoke(messages)
331	    except Exception as exc:
332	        # Prefer the typed HTTP status (ollama.ResponseError / httpx carry one): 404 = the model
333	        # isn't pulled → permanent. Fall back to substring matching only if no status is exposed
334	        # (robust to upstream message-wording changes — audit M4).
335	        status = getattr(exc, "status_code", None) or getattr(
336	            getattr(exc, "response", None), "status_code", None
337	        )
338	        msg = str(exc).lower()
339	        permanent = status == 404 or any(
340	            s in msg for s in ("not found", "no such model", "try pulling")
341	        )
342	        if permanent:
343	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"bulk model {model!r} not available: {exc}") from exc
344	        raise ReasoningError(f"bulk tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
345	    content = getattr(reply, "content", reply)
346	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
347	    text = text.strip()
348	    if not text:  # an empty reply is not a valid result — retry, don't return "" (issue #5/R5)
349	        raise ReasoningError("bulk tier returned an empty reply")
350	    return text
351	
352	
353	def _high_stakes_auth_present() -> bool:
354	    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
355	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
356	    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
357	        return True
358	    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()
359	
360	
361	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
362	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
363	    try:
364	        import anyio
365	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
366	            AssistantMessage,
367	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
368	            ResultMessage,
369	            TextBlock,
370	            query,
371	        )
372	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
373	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
374	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
375	        ) from exc
376	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
377	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
378	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
379	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
380	        )
381	
382	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
383	        system_prompt=system,
384	        model=model,
385	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
386	        setting_sources=[],
387	        allowed_tools=[],
388	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
389	    )
390	
391	    parts: list[str] = []
392	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
393	
394	    async def _drain() -> None:
395	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
396	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
397	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
398	        nonlocal result_error
399	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
400	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
401	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
402	                if rl == "rejected":
403	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
404	                continue
405	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
406	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
407	                continue
408	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
409	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
410	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
411	                        parts.append(block.text)
412	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
413	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
414	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
415	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
416	                ):
417	                    result_error = {
418	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
419	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
420	                    }
421	
422	    try:
423	        anyio.run(_drain)
424	    except Exception as exc:
425	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
426	
427	    if result_error is not None:
428	        status = result_error.get("status")
429	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
430	        if status in (429, 529):
431	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")
432	        if status in (401, 403):
433	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier auth failed ({status}): {detail}")
434	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
435	
436	    text = "".join(parts).strip()
437	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)
438	        raise ReasoningError("high-stakes tier returned an empty reply")
439	    return text
440	
441	
442	def _attempt(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
443	    """Route one request to its tier backend (no retry here)."""
444	    if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
445	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning is disabled (ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING)")
446	    p = request.params
447	    system = p.get("system")
448	    temperature = float(p.get("temperature", 0.0))
449	    model = p.get("model")
450	    json_mode = bool(p.get("_json_mode", False))
451	    seed = p.get("_seed")
452	    json_schema = p.get("json_schema")  # optional caller schema → constrained JSON decoding
453	    if request.tier is StakesTier.BULK:
454	        return cast(
455	            str,
456	            _with_timeout(
457	                lambda: _bulk_complete(
458	                    request.prompt,
459	                    system,
460	                    temperature,
461	                    model or BULK_MODEL,
462	                    json_mode,
463	                    seed=int(seed) if seed is not None else None,
464	                    json_schema=json_schema,
465	                ),
466	                BULK_TIMEOUT + 10,
467	                "bulk tier",
468	            ),
469	        )
470	    if request.tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
471	        # The high-stakes tier runs the Claude CLI subprocess (not the in-process Anthropic SDK,
472	        # so no auto-instrumentor covers it) — emit an explicit OpenInference LLM span so Phoenix
473	        # records the model/provider for this otherwise-untraced tier (#2).
474	        llm_attrs = {
475	            "llm.provider": "anthropic",
476	            "llm.system": "anthropic",
477	            "llm.model_name": str(model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL or "subscription-default"),
478	        }
479	        with _span("reasoning.high_stakes", llm_attrs, kind="LLM"):
480	            return cast(
481	                str,
482	                _with_timeout(
483	                    lambda: _high_stakes_complete(
484	                        request.prompt, system, model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL
485	                    ),
486	                    HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30,
487	                    "high-stakes tier",
488	                ),
489	            )
490	    raise PermanentReasoningError(f"unknown tier: {request.tier!r}")
491	
492	
493	def _sleep_bounded(secs: float, deadline: float | None) -> None:
494	    """Sleep ``secs``, but never past ``deadline`` — so backoff can't blow an overall budget.
495	
496	    If the deadline has already passed, raise instead of sleeping the full interval, so a long
497	    rate-limit backoff cannot keep a caller blocked well beyond its deadline (#102).
498	    """
499	    if deadline is not None:
500	        remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()
501	        if remaining <= 0:
502	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded during backoff")
503	        secs = min(secs, remaining)
504	    time.sleep(secs)
505	
506	
507	def _run_with_retries(request: ReasoningRequest, *, deadline: float | None = None) -> str:
508	    """Execute with independent transient and rate-limit retry budgets.
509	
510	    ``deadline`` (monotonic seconds) bounds the cumulative backoff so the retry budgets cannot
511	    block a caller past its overall deadline (#102).
512	    """
513	    transient = 0
514	    rate = 0
515	    while True:
516	        try:
517	            return _attempt(request)
518	        except PermanentReasoningError:
519	            raise
520	        except RateLimitError:
521	            if rate >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
522	                raise
523	            _sleep_bounded(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate + 1), deadline)
524	            rate += 1
525	        except ReasoningError:
526	            if transient >= MAX_RETRIES:
527	                raise
528	            _sleep_bounded(BACKOFF_BASE**transient, deadline)
529	            transient += 1
530	
531	
532	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
533	# Public seam
534	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
535	@runtime_checkable
536	class ReasoningSeam(Protocol):
537	    """Route a reasoning request to the appropriate tier with timeouts, retries, tracing."""
538	
539	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str: ...
540	
541	
542	@contextmanager
543	def _span(name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any], *, kind: str = "AGENT") -> Iterator[None]:
544	    """Emit an OpenTelemetry span if a provider is registered; a cheap no-op otherwise.
545	
546	    Stamps the OpenInference ``openinference.span.kind`` (default ``AGENT`` — a reasoning call)
547	    so Phoenix classifies the span instead of rendering it UNKNOWN. OpenTelemetry is imported
548	    lazily, so the seam traces when the (self-hosted, on-box) observability stack is wired and is
549	    a silent no-op in tests / offline (ADR-0003).
550	    """
551	    try:
552	        from opentelemetry import trace
553	    except ImportError:
554	        yield
555	        return
556	    tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine.reasoning")
557	    with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
558	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", kind)
559	        for k, v in attributes.items():
560	            span.set_attribute(k, v)
561	        yield
562	
563	
564	class TieredReasoningSeam:
565	    """Concrete tiered seam. ``run`` returns text; ``run_json`` parses a JSON reply.
566	
567	    Backends and tracing are resolved lazily, so an instance constructs with no external
568	    dependencies; calls fail loud (``PermanentReasoningError``) if a tier's backend or its
569	    subscription auth is absent. When the observability stack is present, each call emits an
570	    OpenTelemetry span; otherwise tracing is a no-op.
571	    """
572	
573	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
574	        attrs = {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose}
575	        with _span("reasoning.run", attrs):
576	            return _run_with_retries(request)
577	
578	    def run_json(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> Any:
579	        """Generate and parse JSON, re-rolling on parse failure.
580	
581	        If the caller supplies ``params["json_schema"]``, the BULK tier uses Ollama's
582	        schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path). Otherwise it falls back to loose JSON
583	        mode + prompt instruction. BULK decoding can be deterministic (temp 0), so re-issuing the
584	        identical prompt would yield a byte-identical bad reply; each retry **varies the seed**
585	        (forcing a different deterministic decode) and *monotonically* raises temperature toward
586	        1.0 — never lowering it, so re-rolls never sharpen back toward the same output. ``params``
587	        is frozen, so each attempt builds a fresh request (issue #R6).
588	        """
589	        base_temp = float(request.params.get("temperature", 0.0))
590	        system = request.params.get("system")
591	        json_system = (
592	            f"{system}\n\nRespond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
593	            if system
594	            else "Respond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
595	        )
596	        last: ReasoningError | None = None
597	        deadline = time.monotonic() + RUN_JSON_DEADLINE  # overall bound across all re-rolls (#102)
598	        with _span(
599	            "reasoning.run_json",
600	            {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose},
601	        ):
602	            for attempt in range(JSON_REROLLS + 1):
603	                if attempt > 0 and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
604	                    raise last or ReasoningError("run_json exceeded its overall deadline")
605	                temp = min(1.0, base_temp + 0.2 * attempt)  # monotonic, never decreases (M3)
606	                params = dict(request.params)
607	                params.update(temperature=temp, system=json_system, _json_mode=True, _seed=attempt)
608	                attempt_req = ReasoningRequest(
609	                    prompt=request.prompt,
610	                    tier=request.tier,
611	                    purpose=request.purpose,
612	                    params=params,
613	                )
614	                # A transient/rate/permanent error is already handled (and bounded by the shared
615	                # deadline) inside _run_with_retries — let it PROPAGATE rather than re-rolling, so
616	                # the budgets do NOT multiply (#102). We re-roll ONLY on a JSON *parse* failure.
617	                text = _run_with_retries(attempt_req, deadline=deadline)
618	                try:
619	                    return extract_json(text)
620	                except ReasoningError as exc:  # parse failure → re-roll with a new seed
621	                    last = exc
622	            raise last if last is not None else ReasoningError("run_json failed")
623	
624	    @staticmethod
625	    def is_available(tier: StakesTier) -> bool:
626	        """Cheap liveness check (spends no LLM turn). Never raises — returns False on error."""
627	        if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
628	            return False
629	        try:
630	            if tier is StakesTier.BULK:
631	                require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
632	                import urllib.request
633	
634	                with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{BULK_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=2) as resp:
635	                    return bool(resp.status == 200)
636	            if tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
637	                import shutil
638	
639	                return shutil.which("claude") is not None and _high_stakes_auth_present()
640	        except Exception:
641	            return False
642	        return False
643	
644	
645	class UnconfiguredReasoningSeam:
646	    """Explicit fail-loud seam for contexts that must not perform reasoning."""
647	
648	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
649	        raise NotImplementedError(
650	            "this context uses an UnconfiguredReasoningSeam — supply a TieredReasoningSeam "
651	            "(or a test double) to perform reasoning"
652	        )
653

content.text:
The claim is detailed and specific. Let me analyze the core mechanics carefully.

Key facts confirmed by reading:
1. `_with_timeout` uses `future.result(timeout=...)` but explicitly does NOT cancel the worker (lines 225-229).
2. `_release` (slot decrement) is only fired via `add_done_callback` — i.e., when the worker actually returns.
3. BULK tier: `_bulk_complete` passes `client_kwargs={"timeout": BULK_TIMEOUT}` (line 316) — so the worker is guaranteed to return.
4. HIGH_STAKES tier: `_high_stakes_complete` passes NO timeout to `ClaudeAgentOptions` or `query()`, and wraps in `anyio.run(_drain)` with no cancellation (line 423).

Let me verify the claim that `ClaudeAgentOptions` has no timeout mechanism and that there's nothing else bounding the subprocess. Let me check the installed SDK.

content.text: The SDK has `load_timeout_ms` but no general call timeout option. The claim is correct that `_high_stakes_complete` passes no timeout. Let me look at the existing tests around the timeout/slot leak to understand what behavior is already tested, particularly the test at line 225.

content.content:
180	    assert rc._run_with_retries(_req()) == "ok"
181	    # backpressure sleeps use RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (attempt+1), not the small transient base
182	    assert sleeps == [rc.RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * 1, rc.RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * 2]

184	
185	# ---- timeout pool + saturation guard ----
186	
187	
188	def test_submit_bounded_saturation_is_retryable_backpressure(monkeypatch):
189	    # #104: saturation is transient — must be a retryable RateLimitError (backpressure), not a
190	    # permanent failure that drops the caller with no retry.
191	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "_inflight", rc._POOL_WORKERS)
192	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError, match="saturated"):
193	        rc._submit_bounded(lambda: 42)
194	
195	
196	def test_submit_bounded_normalizes_pool_shutdown(monkeypatch):

198	    # not leak the raw RuntimeError.
199	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "_inflight", 0)
200	
201	    class DeadPool:
202	        def submit(self, fn):
203	            raise RuntimeError("cannot schedule new futures after shutdown")

content.text:
Now I understand the existing test coverage. The tests confirm the mechanism the assessor describes: `test_with_timeout_raises_on_overrun` shows the wall-clock timeout fires but the worker (`_t.sleep(5)`) keeps running and holds the slot until it completes. The saturation guard returns a `RateLimitError`.

Let me write a PoC to validate the central claim: that a hung high-stakes-style call permanently holds slots, and that saturation then breaks an unrelated bulk caller process-wide and is unrecoverable.

input.command:
cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && cat > /tmp/poc_seam.py <<'PY'
import importlib, time, threading
import assay_engine.reasoning.seam as rc

# Speed up rate-limit retries so the "exhaustion" path is observable quickly.
rc.RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = 0.05
rc.RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = 5

# ---- Part 1: simulate 8 HUNG high-stakes-style calls (never return) ----
hang_gate = threading.Event()  # never set -> workers block forever, like a wedged subprocess

# Patch _attempt to mimic high-stakes hanging inside the pooled worker, exactly as
# _with_timeout would run it: submit a fn that blocks, then wall-clock timeout fires
# but the worker keeps the slot.
def hung_high_stakes():
    hang_gate.wait()   # never returns
    return "never"

# Drive the real _with_timeout with a short timeout so the wall-clock fires fast,
# but the worker keeps running (slot leaked) -- identical to the high-stakes no-cancel path.
def fire_hung():
    try:
        rc._with_timeout(hung_high_stakes, 0.2, "high-stakes (hung)")
    except rc.ReasoningError as e:
        pass  # "timed out" -- caller gave up, worker still holds slot

threads = [threading.Thread(target=fire_hung, daemon=True) for _ in range(rc._POOL_WORKERS)]
for t in threads: t.start()
# allow them to be submitted + time out
time.sleep(1.0)
print("after 8 hung high-stakes timeouts: _inflight =", rc._inflight, "/", rc._POOL_WORKERS)

# ---- Part 2: an unrelated, HEALTHY caller (Study B, BULK) tries a fast call ----
def healthy_bulk_attempt(req):
    # bypass real backend: simulate a fast, healthy bulk completion
    return "fast-ok"

# Build a BULK request and run it through the real retry/pool machinery.
# Monkeypatch only the leaf backend so the call itself is healthy; the pool is the bottleneck.
orig_bulk = rc._bulk_complete
rc._bulk_complete = lambda *a, **k: "fast-ok"

req = rc.ReasoningRequest(prompt="hi", tier=rc.StakesTier.BULK, purpose="studyB")
t0 = time.monotonic()
try:
    out = rc._run_with_retries(req)
    print("Study B BULK call result:", out)
except rc.RateLimitError as e:
    print("Study B BULK call FAILED with RateLimitError after %.2fs: %s" % (time.monotonic()-t0, e))
except rc.ReasoningError as e:
    print("Study B BULK call FAILED (%s): %s" % (type(e).__name__, e))

print("final _inflight =", rc._inflight, "(never recovers; workers still hung)")
PY
.venv/bin/python /tmp/poc_seam.py 2>&1 | head -30

content.text: The harness keeps backgrounding because daemon threads block `sleep`-based foreground completion detection. Let me make the PoC self-contained with its own exit and avoid leaving non-daemon hangs.

content.text: Output is empty — the script's stdout is buffered and the process is hung on daemon threads (pool workers never exit, and `_pool` non-daemon threads in ThreadPoolExecutor keep the interpreter alive at exit). Let me add `flush=True`, force `os._exit`, and write results to a file directly.

input.command:
cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && cat > /tmp/poc_seam2.py <<'PY'
import time, threading, os
import assay_engine.reasoning.seam as rc

rc.RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = 0.05
rc.RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = 5

out = open("/tmp/poc_result.txt", "w")
def log(*a):
    print(*a, file=out); out.flush()

hang_gate = threading.Event()  # never set
def hung_high_stakes():
    hang_gate.wait()
    return "never"

def fire_hung():
    try:
        rc._with_timeout(hung_high_stakes, 0.2, "high-stakes (hung)")
    except rc.ReasoningError:
        pass

threads = [threading.Thread(target=fire_hung, daemon=True) for _ in range(rc._POOL_WORKERS)]
for t in threads: t.start()
time.sleep(1.0)
log("after 8 hung high-stakes timeouts: _inflight =", rc._inflight, "/", rc._POOL_WORKERS)

rc._bulk_complete = lambda *a, **k: "fast-ok"
req = rc.ReasoningRequest(prompt="hi", tier=rc.StakesTier.BULK, purpose="studyB")
t0 = time.monotonic()
try:
    res = rc._run_with_retries(req)
    log("Study B BULK call result:", res)
except rc.RateLimitError as e:
    log("Study B BULK FAILED RateLimitError after %.2fs: %s" % (time.monotonic()-t0, e))
except rc.ReasoningError as e:
    log("Study B BULK FAILED %s: %s" % (type(e).__name__, e))

log("final _inflight =", rc._inflight, "(workers still hung -> no recovery)")
out.flush(); out.close()
os._exit(0)
PY
.venv/bin/python /tmp/poc_seam2.py; cat /tmp/poc_result.txt

content.text:
The PoC confirms the assessor's three claims:
1. 8 hung high-stakes-style calls drive `_inflight` to 8/8 and it never returns.
2. An unrelated, healthy BULK call (Study B) fails with `RateLimitError` ("pool saturated") instead of running.
3. `_inflight` stays pinned at 8 — no recovery.

Now I need to verify the critical premise: **can the high-stakes path actually hang indefinitely with no enforced inner timeout?** The assessor claims `_high_stakes_complete` passes no timeout. Confirmed from the code: `ClaudeAgentOptions(...)` at lines 382-389 sets no timeout field, and `anyio.run(_drain)` (line 423) has no `move_on_after`/`fail_after`. The outer `_with_timeout` wraps it with `HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30` but that's the wall-clock-only path that does NOT cancel — exactly the leak.

Let me confirm the contrast with BULK: BULK passes `client_kwargs={"timeout": BULK_TIMEOUT}` so the underlying httpx call IS guaranteed to return, meaning the worker frees its slot. This asymmetry is real. Let me double-check there's no other mechanism (e.g., a watchdog or pool that recycles workers, or the SDK self-terminating on `max_turns`/budget).

content.text:
Confirmed: there is NO reclaim/watchdog/cancellation mechanism anywhere. `query()` itself has no timeout parameter. The only timeout-ish option, `load_timeout_ms`, only bounds the CLI load phase, not the streaming response.

Now, one important nuance to assess for severity/realism: **is `anyio.run(_drain)` actually un-cancellable?** Since the worker is a plain thread (not the main thread), `anyio.run` runs its own event loop in that thread. If the high-stakes path were wrapped with `with anyio.move_on_after(HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT): ...` inside `_drain`, anyio would cancel the async generator and the SDK's transport would terminate the subprocess — making the worker return and free the slot. The fix is feasible and the absence of it is the defect.

Let me verify that BULK's protection is genuinely robust (so this is truly a high-stakes-specific gap, not a general one), and confirm the high-stakes outer `_with_timeout` is the only bound.

The analysis is complete. Let me verify there isn't a separate higher-level deadline that would terminate the subprocess. The `run_study` orchestration — does it run high-stakes calls in a way that the subprocess is killed on study teardown? Let me check quickly.

content.text:
No higher-level subprocess lifecycle management exists. The seam is the sole owner of high-stakes execution and there's no kill path.

My assessment is complete. The finding is REAL and well-supported.

Summary of verification:
- **Mechanism confirmed in code**: `_with_timeout` (lines 220-229) enforces wall-clock-only timeout via `future.result(timeout=...)` and explicitly does NOT cancel the worker. `_release` only fires via `add_done_callback` when the worker actually returns.
- **Asymmetry confirmed**: BULK (`_bulk_complete`, line 316) passes `client_kwargs={"timeout": BULK_TIMEOUT}`, guaranteeing the worker returns. HIGH_STAKES (`_high_stakes_complete`, lines 382-423) passes NO timeout to `ClaudeAgentOptions` or `query()`, and `anyio.run(_drain)` has no `move_on_after`/`fail_after`. SDK `query()` has no timeout parameter (verified against installed SDK 0.2.102; `load_timeout_ms` only bounds CLI load, not streaming).
- **PoC confirmed all three sub-claims**: 8 hung high-stakes-style timeouts pin `_inflight` at 8/8 permanently; a healthy unrelated BULK call (Study B) then fails with `RateLimitError` ("pool saturated"); `_inflight` never recovers.
- **Cross-study, process-wide, unrecoverable**: `_pool` and `_inflight` are module-global, shared across all `run_study` invocations. No watchdog/reclaim/recycle anywhere (grep confirmed).

Severity calibration: The trigger requires the claude subprocess to genuinely wedge (stalled subscription endpoint, network blackhole, child wedged) 8 times. This is plausible but not the everyday path — most stalls eventually error or the SDK transport closes. It's a real liveness/availability defect that requires a process restart to recover, affecting all concurrent studies. HIGH is the right severity (not CRITICAL: it needs a specific external failure mode to manifest, and BULK-only deployments are unaffected; not MEDIUM: when it does trigger it's a total, unrecoverable reasoning outage across the whole process).

input.evidence:
CODE: src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py.
- _with_timeout (220-229): `future.result(timeout=timeout)`; on FuturesTimeout it does NOT call future.cancel() and the docstring (226-229) states a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread blocked in a backend call — the worker keeps its slot.
- _release (186-189) decrements _inflight ONLY via add_done_callback (216), i.e. when the worker actually returns.
- _submit_bounded (192-217) raises RateLimitError once _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS (8). _pool/_inflight are module-global (180-183), shared across all run_study invocations.
- BULK is safe: _bulk_complete (316) passes client_kwargs={'timeout': BULK_TIMEOUT}, so the worker is guaranteed to return.
- HIGH_STAKES leaks: _high_stakes_complete (361-439) builds ClaudeAgentOptions (382-389) with NO timeout field and runs anyio.run(_drain) (423) with no move_on_after/fail_after. The outer _with_timeout uses HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT+30 (486) but that is the no-cancel wall-clock path. SDK query() (verified, claude-agent-sdk 0.2.102) has no timeout param; only load_timeout_ms exists and bounds CLI load, not streaming. grep found NO watchdog/reclaim/recycle/terminate anywhere in src/.

PoC (/tmp/poc_seam2.py, run under .venv): drove the REAL _with_timeout with 8 never-returning workers + short timeout (the exact high-stakes no-cancel semantics). Output:
  after 8 hung high-stakes timeouts: _inflight = 8 / 8
  Study B BULK FAILED RateLimitError after 1.33s: reasoning timeout pool saturated (8/8 in-flight) — backpressure; retry shortly
  final _inflight = 8 (workers still hung -> no recovery)
This reproduces all three assessor claims: pool pinned at 8/8 permanently; an unrelated healthy BULK study fails with RateLimitError; no recovery.

Existing tests corroborate the mechanism: test_with_timeout_raises_on_overrun (test_reasoning.py:225) shows the timeout fires while the worker (sleep(5)) keeps running and holds its slot.
input.reasoning: TRUE production-readiness defect, independently confirmed by reading the code and by a working PoC. The reasoning seam routes every call through one module-global 8-worker ThreadPoolExecutor whose slots are only released when the worker returns. The wall-clock _with_timeout deliberately does not cancel the worker. The BULK tier is protected because it hands a real client_kwargs timeout to ChatOllama, guaranteeing the worker returns and frees its slot. The HIGH_STAKES tier passes NO enforceable timeout to ClaudeAgentOptions/query() and wraps the claude subprocess in anyio.run(_drain) with no cancellation, so a wedged subprocess (stalled subscription endpoint, network blackhole, wedged child) blocks the worker forever and permanently leaks its pool slot. Because _pool/_inflight are process-global and shared across all concurrent run_study invocations, 8 such hangs saturate the pool permanently: _submit_bounded then raises RateLimitError for EVERY subsequent call of ANY tier in ANY study, _run_with_retries exhausts its rate budget and fails the caller, and there is no recovery short of a process restart. This is a genuine cross-study, process-wide, unrecoverable availability defect of the most critical tier; not a pass-1 finding (#101-#122) and not a documented/justified scope item (the docstring claims 'hard per-call timeouts via a bounded worker pool', which the high-stakes tier does not actually enforce). Severity HIGH rather than CRITICAL because manifestation requires a specific external failure mode (the claude subprocess wedging without erroring) recurring up to the pool ceiling, and pure BULK-only deployments are unaffected; HIGH rather than MEDIUM because when it does trigger the impact is a total, unrecoverable reasoning outage across all concurrent studies. The suggested fix is sound and feasible: wrap _drain under anyio.fail_after/move_on_after bounded by HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT so the SDK transport terminates the subprocess and the worker RETURNS (firing _release); optionally isolate high-stakes in its own bounded pool and/or surface a metric when _inflight pins at _POOL_WORKERS.

content.text: Verified the finding as a TRUE production-readiness defect at HIGH severity. The high-stakes tier in `/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py` (lines 361-439, with the leak mechanism at 220-229 and pool at 180-217) lacks an enforceable inner timeout, allowing a wedged claude subprocess to permanently leak global pool slots and break reasoning process-wide across all concurrent studies. PoC confirmed all three of the assessor's claims.
